name: Human Platelet Lysate (hPL)
vendor_examples: UltraGRO (Helios Biosciences);PLTMax (Mill Creek BioSolutions);Stemulate (Cook Regentec);PLUS (Atlantis Bioscience)
application_scope: General cell culture; chemosensitivity assays; MSC expansion; endothelial culture
notes: Use at 5% hPL as equivalent to 10% FBS in most protocols. Rauch et al ALTEX 2011 validated for anchorage-dependent and -independent human and animal cell lines. Pons et al ALTEX 2019 validated for chemosensitivity assays. NOT validated as drop-in for all cancer cell lines — check FCS-free database (Utrecht University) for your specific line.
source_citation: Rauch et al ALTEX 2011 DOI:10.14573/altex.2011.4.305; Pons et al ALTEX 2019 DOI:10.14573/altex.1809211; Peters et al Biomed Res Int 2022 DOI:10.1155/2022/3807314

name: Serum-Free Defined Medium
vendor_examples: mTeSR1 (STEMCELL Technologies cat#05850);StemFlex (Gibco);StemMACS iPS-Brew (Miltenyi);B27+N2 supplement system (Gibco)
application_scope: Stem cell maintenance; iPSC-CM differentiation and maturation
notes: Fully defined; best reproducibility. Requires adaptation period 2-4 passages for lines previously grown in FBS. B27+RPMI-1640 is the standard serum-free protocol for iPSC-CM per Burridge et al 2014. Not suitable as general FBS drop-in for cancer cell lines.
source_citation: STEMCELL Technologies mTeSR1 protocol; Burridge et al Nature Methods 2014 DOI:10.1038/nmeth.2999

name: Plant-Based Hydrolysates
application_scope: Biopharmaceutical production; not validated for primary research-grade assays
notes: Lowest cost animal-free option. Variable performance across cell lines. Best validated for CHO and HEK293 in bioproduction context not research assays. Requires significant protocol optimization.
source_citation: Kerry HyPep technical documentation; Chelladurai et al Heliyon 2021 DOI:10.1016/j.heliyon.2021.e07686

name: TrypLE Express (recombinant fungal protease)
vendor_examples: TrypLE Express (Gibco cat#12604013);TrypLE Select (Gibco cat#A1285901);CTS TrypLE Select (Gibco — GMP grade)
application_scope: All adherent mammalian cell dissociation; GMP cell therapy manufacturing
notes: Direct replacement for bovine or porcine trypsin with no protocol changes required per Gibco product documentation and Nestler et al 2004. Recombinant fungal enzyme produced by microbial fermentation — animal origin-free. Better temperature stability than animal trypsin: stable 12+ months at room temperature vs porcine trypsin which loses 95% activity in 24h at 37C. FDA-preferred for GMP manufacturing.
source_citation: Nestler et al 2004 Semantic Scholar DOI — Gibco TrypLE Express product documentation cat#12604013; Gibco TrypLE Express & Select product page verified 2026-06-24

name: Accutase (protease/collagenase blend)
application_scope: iPSC passaging; primary cell dissociation; sensitive cell types
notes: Contains proteolytic and collagenolytic enzymes from microbial sources. Gentler than trypsin for sensitive primary cells. Not animal-origin-free in all formulations — verify CoA. STEMCELL Technologies version confirmed animal-component-free.
source_citation: STEMCELL Technologies Accutase product documentation cat#07920 verified 2026-06-24

name: PEG Synthetic Hydrogel (research-grade)
vendor_examples: PEG hydrogels (custom formulation per Murphy lab protocol);VitroGel (TheWell Bioscience);GrowDex (UPM Biomedicals)
application_scope: iPSC-CM differentiation; 2D cell culture coating; drug screening substrates
notes: PEG hydrogels showed 24% higher cTnT expression and greater reproducibility than Matrigel for iPSC-CM differentiation per RSC Biomaterials Science 2025. HOWEVER: GrowDex and VitroGel did NOT support vascular organoid survival in Nature Scientific Reports 2025 — performance is application-specific. Use for iPSC-CM; verify independently for other applications.
source_citation: RSC Biomaterials Science 2025 DOI:10.1039/d4bm01636j; Nature Scientific Reports 2025 DOI:10.1038/s41598-025-20091-w; Aisenbrey & Murphy Nature Reviews Materials 2020 DOI:10.1038/s41578-020-0199-8

name: Recombinant Laminin Fragments
vendor_examples: iMatrix-511 (Nippi/ReproCELL cat#892012);Laminin-521 (BioLamina cat#LN521);LN-511 (BioLamina cat#LN511);Vitronectin XF (STEMCELL Technologies cat#07180)
application_scope: iPSC maintenance coating; iPSC-CM culture; xeno-free workflows
notes: Fully human recombinant — no mouse or animal origin. iMatrix-511 (Laminin-511 E8 fragment) is the most widely validated drop-in for iPSC culture. Vitronectin XF validated as xeno-free Matrigel replacement for iPSC coating. Higher cost than Matrigel but fully defined and consistent.
source_citation: Nippi iMatrix-511 product documentation; STEMCELL Technologies Vitronectin XF protocol; Rodin et al Nature Communications 2014 DOI:10.1038/ncomms4195

name: Fibrin Hydrogel (human recombinant)
application_scope: 3D organoid culture; vascular differentiation; scaffold for 3D cardiac constructs
notes: Validated as Matrigel replacement for hiPSC-derived vascular organoids with no significant differences in differentiation efficacy per Nature Scientific Reports 2025. Protocol: 7.5mg/mL fibrinogen + 0.1 U/mL thrombin. Requires protocol optimization but fully defined and human-derived.

name: Recombinant Human Serum Albumin (rHSA)
application_scope: Cell culture supplement; cryopreservation; drug formulation stabilizer
notes: Global market price ~$62.6/gram in 2024. Recombumin (Sartorius/Albumedix) is the most widely validated — used in Heartseed HS-001 investigational cardiac cell therapy (March 2023). Structurally equivalent to plasma-derived HSA. Rice-derived (Pichia pastoris expression) — no animal components. Drop-in replacement for BSA in most applications.
source_citation: Market research verified global price ~$62.6/gram 2024; Recombumin cardiac cell therapy application March 2023 per Albumedix press release

name: Recombinant Human Insulin
vendor_examples: Thermo Fisher 12585-014 (rh insulin 10mg/mL);Sigma-Aldrich I9278 (human recombinant);Novo Nordisk Actrapid (pharmaceutical grade)
application_scope: Cell culture insulin supplement; differentiation media; ATCC-specified for MCF-7
notes: ATCC specifically lists Thermo cat#12585-014 recombinant human insulin for MCF-7 (HTB-22) — not bovine. Biosynthetic human insulin sequence. Fully recombinant — no animal origin. Direct drop-in for all bovine insulin applications.
source_citation: ATCC HTB-22 product sheet specifying Thermo 12585-014; Thermo Fisher recombinant insulin product documentation verified 2026-06-24

name: Recombinant Human Collagen Type I
vendor_examples: FibroGen rhCollagen Type I;Collgard (BioTissue);CELLMATRIX Type I-A (Nitta Gelatin)
validated_cell_lines: Fibroblasts;primary cells;3D culture models;cardiac tissue engineering
application_scope: 3D cell culture matrix; tissue engineering scaffold; primary cell attachment
notes: Human sequence — higher specificity for human cells than bovine collagen. Higher cost. Requires protocol optimization if switching from established bovine collagen protocols.
source_citation: FibroGen recombinant collagen product documentation; CELLMATRIX product documentation

name: Gelatin Methacryloyl (GelMA)
application_scope: Photocrosslinkable 3D matrix; cardiac tissue engineering; bioprinting
notes: Photocrosslinkable with UV — tunable stiffness. Patel et al Int J Mol Sci 2023 reviews GelMA for iPSC-CM applications. Does not replicate native collagen fibril structure. Lower cost than recombinant collagen but different mechanical properties.
source_citation: Patel et al Int J Mol Sci 2023 DOI:10.3390/ijms24129995; Bertlein et al Advanced Materials 2017

name: Recombinant Human Fibronectin
vendor_examples: Corning recombinant human fibronectin (cat#356008);Sigma-Aldrich F0895 (human plasma — note: still plasma-derived, verify);R&D Systems 1918-FN
application_scope: Cell attachment coating; primary CM culture substrate; wound healing assays
notes: NOTE: Sigma F0895 is plasma-derived from human plasma — not animal-free but human-derived. Corning cat#356008 is recombinant. Verify supplier carefully. Drop-in for coating applications at same concentration as bovine fibronectin.
source_citation: Corning recombinant fibronectin cat#356008 product documentation; R&D Systems 1918-FN product documentation verified 2026-06-24

name: In Vitro Produced Monoclonal Antibodies
vendor_examples: Custom hybridoma serum-free culture (various CROs);CHO-expressed recombinant antibodies;phage display libraries
application_scope: All antibody applications where ascites-produced antibodies were previously used
notes: In vitro hybridoma culture now standard for most commercial mAb production. Phage display and CHO expression for fully recombinant antibodies. Ascites method increasingly restricted in EU/UK. Verify with supplier whether your specific antibody is ascites-derived before logging animal impact.
source_citation: Zhiqiang An Therapeutic Monoclonal Antibodies 2009 Wiley; 3R guidance on antibody production ECVAM
